title: brain query <text> — --json success output
description: Tier-0 hybrid retrieval, optionally + grounded answer. Resolves the query through the layer precedence in retrieval-index-contract.md §5 (exact id → slug → unique alias → fts/vector RRF fusion), packs a section-aware context, and — unless --no-answer — returns a generated answer citing note ids. Retrieval runs only over the SQLite-active generation, filtered by notes.active_generation_id (§2). The response is a discriminated union on `mode`: `answered` (default) generates an answer and records a model_calls row; `retrieval-only` (--no-answer) returns ranked items only and records NO model_calls. Either mode records retrieval_runs/retrieval_results via finalizeLedgerWrite and emits exactly one run.readonly git-ref audit event; neither makes any canonical/worktree/projection mutation. A post-run backup applies (it wrote ledger rows). Exit 0 on success.

mode.description: `answered` (default) generates and returns an answer (and records a model_calls row); `retrieval-only` (--no-answer) skips generation entirely — no `answer`, no `modelCalls`, no model_calls ledger side effect.

answer.description: The generated answer, grounded in the packed context and citing note ids. Present iff mode=answered; may be empty when no context met the retrieval threshold. Absent in retrieval-only mode.

truncated.description: Present (always `true`) iff mode=answered AND the provider cut the answer short (finish reason other than STOP, e.g. MAX_TOKENS) — the `answer` is a fragment, not a complete grounded answer. Absent when the answer completed normally or in retrieval-only mode.

modelCalls.description: Number of model_calls ledger rows recorded by the generation step. Present iff mode=answered; absent in retrieval-only mode (generation is skipped, so no model_calls row is written).

items.description: The ranked retrieval results backing the answer, one entry PER NOTE (chunks are folded to their owning note per §5), in fused (RRF) rank order. Ordered by descending `score`, ties broken by ascending `noteId`.

noteId.description: The candidate note (the RRF candidate unit — chunks are folded to notes, §5).

sectionPath.description: Heading breadcrumb of the best-ranked matching section for this note (§1); the chunk that supplied the note's best per-layer rank.

score.description: Fused RRF score (§5): the sum of every entry's `weightedContribution`. For an identity short-circuit it is that layer's deterministic score.

contributions.description: Per-layer provenance (Task 3.3): every layer that surfaced this note, with its folded 1-based rank and weighted RRF contribution. An FTS+vector hit lists both; an identity short-circuit lists exactly one identity layer. `Σ weightedContribution == score`.

layer.description: The layer that surfaced this note. Identity layers short-circuit; fts/vector are the fused statistical layers.

rank.description: This note's folded 1-based rank within the layer (best chunk rank, re-densified per §5). Identity short-circuits report rank 1.

weightedContribution.description: weight[layer] / (k + rank) for statistical layers (§5); the deterministic score for an identity short-circuit.

citation.description: True when this item is cited by the answer.

layersUsed.description: Which retrieval layers participated for this query (fts absent when the FTS-maturity fallback is active — contract §6).

degraded.description: True when the LanceDB FTS-maturity fallback (contract §6) dropped the fts layer for this query.

examples.answer: Atlas is the second-brain system described in [[concept-atlas]].

x-atlas-contract.summary: Hybrid retrieval (layer precedence + RRF fusion per retrieval-index-contract.md) → packed context → grounded, cited answer; audited Tier-0 read.

flags.name: --k <n>
flags.description: Number of ranked items to retrieve/fuse before context packing.

flags.name: --type <noteType>
flags.description: Metadata filter: restrict retrieval to a note type.

flags.name: --no-answer
flags.description: Retrieval-only mode: return ranked items only (response mode=retrieval-only), skipping the generation step entirely — no `answer`, no `modelCalls`, and no model_calls ledger row. Default (mode=answered) generates a grounded answer.

sideEffects: records a model_calls row ONLY in mode=answered; --no-answer (mode=retrieval-only) skips generation and writes no model_calls row
emits exactly one run.readonly git-ref audit event (executed Tier-0 read)
a post-run backup applies (this run wrote ledger rows)
prohibitedEffects: no retrieval over a non-active index generation
no more than one terminal run.readonly event per run

errorCodes.when: the SQLite store file is missing or unopenable
the LanceDB index is not configured/present so no statistical layer can run

the fail-closed backup watermark is blocked (ledger-backup-contract.md §5): query writes retrieval_runs/retrieval_results via finalizeLedgerWrite, so BOTH modes (answered and retrieval-only) are refused when the watermark is blocked — a ledger-writing run is fail-closed until a verified backup catches up
the query normalizes to more than one note's alias (identity layer refuses a silent pick)
the query embed (or answer generation) hit a provider-retryable ProviderError — rate_limit/quota/timeout/transport; envelope carries retryable:true and retryAfterMs when the provider supplied timing
the query embed failed with a non-retryable ProviderError (authentication/model_incompatible/embedding-dimension mismatch)
